summary: Rewrote the right selected-district inspector into a decision assistant. The panel now leads with current problem, recommendation, cost, benefit, risk, next action, and translated reason chips before showing grouped read-model data. Existing population, labor, grain, cash, route, governance, obligation, appointment, and campaign data remain available under decision data sections. Actions still call the existing appointment/campaign/obligation preview and confirmation flows; no sim-core, protocol, or domain-rule changes were made.

decisions: Decision assistant is a presentation-only derivation over existing read models; it does not create a UI rule authority or mutate WorldState.
The first visible inspector block now uses player labels for current problem, recommendation, cost, benefit, risk, and next action before resource metrics.
Raw district read-model values remain visible but are grouped as decision data: resources, route/campaign, governance/appointment, effects, and actions.

Reason chips continue through i18n.formatReasonCode; additional stable reason keys were added for route, campaign, grain, muster, governance, obligation, siege, withdrawal, and postwar codes used by the inspector and M4 panels.
Existing appointment preview/confirm and M4 command callbacks were reused; no new production dependency or protocol surface was introduced.
risks: The recommendation priority is intentionally shallow and read-model based; gameplay review should verify whether obligation-first versus appointment-first ordering matches the desired player guidance.
The i18n catalog now translates more M4 reason codes, so tests that intentionally expected fallback title-case text may need to assert player-facing copy instead.
No Electron files were touched, so no new Electron security review trigger was introduced by this diff.
requested_action: Review the decision-inspector rewrite for player comprehension and UX priority, then route to test_engineer/qa_reviewer for independent validation. Do not mark accepted until reviewer approval.
